strengths: Proven full-stack expertise across TypeScript, Node.js, React, and Nest.js equivalents, with direct experience building scalable internal tooling and automation platforms at re:cinq and Optilyz.
Demonstrated mastery in AI-agent infrastructure development and MCP code generation, directly aligning with the required AI agent development skill and enterprise tooling focus.

Extensive track record of owning end-to-end systems — from frontend UIs to backend APIs, cloud deployments (AWS CDK), and DevOps tooling (Docker, GitLab CI) — with a strong emphasis on testing, documentation, and developer experience.
concerns: Limited explicit mention of Vitest or Nest.js in resume, though equivalent testing (Jest, custom runners) and framework experience (Node.js, GraphQL) are abundant.
German language proficiency is listed as basic, which may pose minor communication challenges in a Berlin-based enterprise environment.
Resume spans many technologies and roles; while demonstrating adaptability, it requires careful interpretation to confirm sustained depth in the required primary stack (TypeScript/Node.js/React).
response.justification: Bogdan’s resume presents a rare combination of deep full-stack engineering experience with direct alignment to the role’s core mandate: building internal automation tooling at scale. His work at re:cinq on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators directly satisfies the required AI agent development skill, while his history of shipping complex, user-facing systems — from Direct Mail automation to open-source GIS platforms — demonstrates consistent end-to-end ownership using the primary stack. His relentless focus on testing, CI/CD, and developer tooling, coupled with hands-on cloud and containerization experience, makes him not just a fit, but a potential force multiplier for engineering quality and process maturity.
